overview: These are vibrant orange lichens, often known as Sunburst Lichens (Xanthoria species) or Elegant Sunburst Lichens (Rusavskia elegans). They grow slowly, sometimes only 0.5 mm per year, and thrive on prominent rocks, particularly those enriched by bird droppings. The lichen is common in Arctic and Antarctic regions, such as Svalbard, where it can tolerate extreme weather. Friends of Kananaskis Country +2 3 sites Spitsbergen lichens and flowering plants. These are vibrant orange lichens, often known as Sunburst Lichens (Xanthoria species) or Elegant Sunburst Lichens (Rusavskia elegans).
They grow slowly, sometimes only 0.5 mm per year, and thrive on prominent rocks, particularly those enriched by bird droppings.
The lichen is common in Arctic and Antarctic regions, such as Svalbard, where it can tolerate extreme weather.
